core_scenarios.description: Greenfield complete flow + profile deposition. User runs discover→spec→build on a new project. After build completes, profile writer extracts information from all stage artifacts and writes to .nopilot/profile/ (L0-L3). If L2 intent capture is enabled, design philosophy and decision rationale are also captured. Profile is available for subsequent feature additions.

Add feature to existing project with profile. User runs /discover, system detects profile, prompts greenfield/feature choice. User selects feature mode. System reads profile for context + scans code structure. User describes feature. System does lightweight requirement convergence: inherits philosophy/constraints/tech from profile, skips direction selection and philosophy extraction, detects conflicts with existing decisions, generates regression guard ACs. Produces specs/features/feat-xxx/discover.json. Continues through spec (with code awareness + Critic impact audit) → build (worktree from existing code) → profile update.

First-time onboarding of existing codebase (no profile). User runs /discover in a project with existing code but no profile. System detects code exists + no profile → triggers first-time onboarding: scans codebase to generate L0/L1/L3 profile, then asks user to choose greenfield (full project-level discover) or feature mode. This prevents treating a single feature's design philosophy as the entire project's philosophy.
